sections.paragraph:
διαγγέλλω fut. 2 pl. διαγγελεῖτε Lev 25:9; 2 aor. 3 sg. subj. διαγγείλῃ Jos 6:10, 2 aor. pass. διηγγέλην, subj. διαγγελῶ (s. ἀγγέλλω; Pind., Thu. et al.; PSI 329, 4; 559, 5; PPetr II, 1, 12 [all III b.c.]; LXX, Philo, Joseph.)

① to make someth. known far and wide, proclaim, spread the news concerning/about (Pind., N. 5, 3 in imagery of the poet’s sweet song speeding in every available vessel to announce a certain boy’s victory in the pancratium; Demosth. 12, 16; Jos., Vi. 98; Just., D. 60, 3) the reign of God Lk 9:60. τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ θεοῦ Ro 9:17 (Ex 9:16). Cp. Mk 5:19 v.l.

② to make a report, announce, report (X., An. 1, 6, 2; Jos., Ant. 7, 201) completion of the days of purification Ac 21:26. Of the church δ. καιρούς announce seasons (fulfillment of prophecies) Dg 11:5. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. pl. = plural

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et et al. = et alii (and others)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

  2. = Nestle/Aland, Novum Testamentum Graece, 27th ed. Other editions are indicated by a superscript—List 1, beg.
  3. = neuter; note (in bibliographical references)

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 227
